term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) pain, agony, sorrow, a writhing, anguish
1a) writhing (of fear)
1b) anguish
term_inventory_hebrew.also_spelled: chi.lah (H2427B — variant form used in source header as separate entry; treated as one term here)

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) covering
1a) covering, obstinacy, blindness (of the heart)
faintness, dismay, failure of mental energy

1) badness, evil
1a) badness, bad quality
1b) wilfulness
1c) evil, badness (ethical)
1d) sadness

1) trouble, wickedness, sorrow
1a) trouble, sorrow
1b) idolatry
1c) trouble of iniquity, wickedness
Also means: a.ven — trouble (H0205H)
term_inventory_hebrew.also_spelled: H0205H (a.ven — trouble; variant entry in STEP)

term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) heavy, great
1a) heavy
1b) massive, abundant, numerous
1c) heavy, dull
1d) hard, difficult, burdensome
1e) very oppressive, numerous, rich

1) mourning
1a) for the dead
1b) for rites of mourning (metaph)
1c) mourning garb
1d) period of mourning

term_inventory_greek.meaning: (passive) to be deeply distressed, grieved with; to be grieved together with; to be grieved, distressed (Mk. 3:5)
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: sullupeō: hurt or mortify together; passive — share in grief, sympathize or condole; tis soi -ēthēsetai (LXX)

term_inventory_greek.meaning: labor, birth pain; agony (of death); the spasms or pains of a woman in travail, a birth pang (1Thess. 5:3); plural metaphorically — birth throes, preliminary troubles to the development of a catastrophe (Mt. 24:8; Mk. 13:8); from the Hebrew, a stringent band, a snare, noose (Acts 2:24)
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: odin (odis): mostly plural — pangs or throes of childbirth; metaphorically — travail, anguish; odines thanatou, hadou — the bonds of death (LXX, due to confusion of Hebrew hebel 'pang' with hebel 'cord')

verse_records.verse_text: The peoples have heard; they tremble; pangs have seized the inhabitants of Philistia.
This would be my comfort; I would even exult in pain unsparing, for I have not denied the words of the Holy One.
Trembling took hold of them there, anguish as of a woman in labor.
We have heard the report of it; our hands fall helpless; anguish has taken hold of us, pain as of a woman in labor.
O inhabitant of Lebanon, nested among the cedars, how you will be pitied when pangs come upon you, pain as of a woman in labor!
The king of Babylon heard the report of them, and his hands fell helpless; anguish seized him, pain as of a woman in labor.
Now why do you cry aloud? Is there no king in you? Has your counselor perished, that pain seized you like a woman in labor?
No, but we did it from fear that in time to come your children might say to our children, 'What have you to do with the Lord, the God of Israel?'
Anxiety in a man's heart weighs him down, but a good word makes him glad.
Concerning Damascus: 'Hamath and Arpad are confounded, for they have heard bad news; they melt in fear, they are troubled like the sea that cannot be quiet.'
Moreover, he said to me, 'Son of man, behold, I will break the supply of bread in Jerusalem. They shall eat bread by weight and with anxiety, and they shall drink water by measure and in dismay.'
'Son of man, eat your bread with quaking, and drink water with trembling and with anxiety.'

And say to the people of the land, Thus says the Lord God concerning the inhabitants of Jerusalem in the land of Israel: They shall eat their bread with anxiety, and drink water in dismay. In this way her land will be stripped of all it contains, on account of the violence of all those who dwell in it.
And he looked around at them with anger, grieved at their hardness of heart, and said to the man, 'Stretch out your hand.' He stretched it out, and his hand was restored.
You will give them dullness of heart; your curse will be on them.
In his neck abides strength, and terror dances before him.

And out of the ground the Lord God made to spring up every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food. The tree of life was in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat, for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die.
For God knows that when you eat of it your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing good and evil.
Then the Lord God said, 'Behold, the man has become like one of us in knowing good and evil. Now, lest he reach out his hand and take also of the tree of life and eat, and live forever—'
The Lord saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth, and that every intention of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually.

And when the Lord smelled the pleasing aroma, the Lord said in his heart, 'I will never again curse the ground because of man, for the intention of man's heart is evil from his youth. Neither will I ever again strike down every living creature as I have done.'
Behold, your servant has found favor in your sight, and you have shown me great kindness in saving my life. But I cannot escape to the hills, lest the disaster overtake me and I die.

These are the generations of Jacob. Joseph, being seventeen years old, was pasturing the flock with his brothers. He was a boy with the sons of Bilhah and Zilpah, his father's wives. And Joseph brought a bad report of them to their father.
So he asked Pharaoh's officers who were with him in custody in his master's house, 'Why are your faces downcast today?'
If you take this one also from me, and harm happens to him, you will bring down my gray hairs in evil to Sheol.

And Jacob said to Pharaoh, 'The days of the years of my sojourning are 130 years. Few and evil have been the days of the years of my life, and they have not attained to the days of the years of the life of my fathers in the days of their sojourning.'
When they are diminished and brought low through oppression, evil, and sorrow,
All the days of the afflicted are evil, but the cheerful of heart has a continual feast.
Whoever sings songs to a heavy heart is like one who takes off a garment on a cold day, and like vinegar on soda.
Sorrow is better than laughter, for by sadness of face the heart is made glad.
I form light and create darkness, I make well-being and create calamity; I am the Lord, who does all these things.

And do you seek great things for yourself? Seek them not, for behold, I am bringing disaster upon all flesh, declares the Lord. But I will give you your life as a prize of war in all places to which you may go.
He has not beheld misfortune in Jacob, nor has he seen trouble in Israel. The Lord their God is with them, and the shout of a king is among them.

I have not eaten of the tithe while I was mourning, or removed any of it while I was unclean, or offered any of it to the dead. I have obeyed the voice of the Lord my God. I have done according to all that you have commanded me.
For rebellion is as the sin of divination, and presumption is as iniquity and idolatry. Because you have rejected the word of the Lord, he has also rejected you from being king.
As I have seen, those who plow iniquity and sow trouble reap the same.
For affliction does not come from the dust, nor does trouble sprout from the ground,
because of the noise of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked. For they drop trouble upon me, and in anger they bear a grudge against me.
The years of our life are seventy, or even by reason of strength eighty; yet their span is but toil and trouble; they are soon gone, and we fly away.
No ill befalls the righteous, but the wicked are filled with trouble.
Woe to those who decree iniquitous decrees, and the writers who keep writing oppression,
No one enters suit justly; no one goes to law honestly; they rely on empty pleas, they speak lies, they conceive mischief and give birth to iniquity.
When they came to the threshing floor of Atad, which is beyond the Jordan, they lamented there with a very great and grievous lamentation, and he made a mourning for his father seven days.

When the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites, saw the mourning on the threshing floor of Atad, they said, 'This is a grievous mourning by the Egyptians.' Therefore the place was named Abel-mizraim; it is beyond the Jordan.
But Moses said to the Lord, 'Oh, my Lord, I am not eloquent, either in the past or since you have spoken to your servant, but I am slow of speech and of tongue.'
Then the Lord said to Moses, 'Pharaoh's heart is hardened; he refuses to let the people go.'
You and the people with you will certainly wear yourselves out, for the thing is too heavy for you. You are not able to do it alone.
I am not able to carry all this people alone; the burden is too heavy for me.
'Your father made our yoke heavy. Now therefore lighten the hard service of your father and his heavy yoke on us, and we will serve you.'
For my iniquities have gone over my head; like a heavy burden, they are too heavy for me.

Ah, sinful nation, a people laden with iniquity, offspring of evildoers, children who deal corruptly! They have forsaken the Lord, they have despised the Holy One of Israel, they are utterly estranged.
For you are not sent to a people of foreign speech and a hard language, but to the house of Israel—
not to many peoples of foreign speech and a hard language, whose words you cannot understand. Surely, if I sent you to such, they would listen to you.
My appetite refuses to touch them; they are as food that is loathsome to me.
The Lord sustains him on his sickbed; in his illness you restore him to full health.
All these are but the beginning of the birth pains.
For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places; there will be famines. These are but the beginning of the birth pains.
God raised him up, loosing the pangs of death, because it was not possible for him to be held by it.
While people are saying, 'There is peace and security,' then sudden destruction will come upon them as labor pains come upon a pregnant woman, and they will not escape.
Yet I will distress Ariel, and there shall be moaning and lamentation, and she shall be to me like an Ariel.

The Lord has become like an enemy; he has swallowed up Israel; he has swallowed up all its palaces; he has laid in ruins its strongholds, and he has multiplied in the daughter of Judah mourning and lamentation.
Now Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing with which his father had blessed him, and Esau said to himself, 'The days of mourning for my father are approaching; then I will kill my brother Jacob.'
When they came to the threshing floor of Atad, which is beyond the Jordan, they lamented there with a very great and grievous lamentation, and he made a mourning for his father seven days.

When the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites, saw the mourning on the threshing floor of Atad, they said, 'This is a grievous mourning by the Egyptians.' Therefore the place was named Abel-mizraim; it is beyond the Jordan.
And the people of Israel wept for Moses in the plains of Moab thirty days. Then the days of weeping and mourning for Moses were ended.
And when the mourning was over, David sent and brought her to his house, and she became his wife and bore him a son. But the thing that David had done displeased the Lord.
So the victory that day was turned into mourning for all the people, for the people heard that day, 'The king is grieving for his son.'

And in every province, wherever the king's command and his decree reached, there was great mourning among the Jews, with fasting and weeping and lamenting, and many of them lay in sackcloth and ashes.

as the days on which the Jews got relief from their enemies, and as the month that had been turned for them from sorrow into gladness and from mourning into a holiday; that they should make them days of feasting and gladness, days for sending gifts of food to one another and gifts to the poor.
My lyre is turned to mourning, and my pipe to the voice of those who weep.
It is better to go to the house of mourning than to go to the house of feasting, for this is the end of all mankind, and the living will lay it to heart.
The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning, but the heart of fools is in the house of mirth.
Your sun shall no more go down, nor your moon withdraw itself; for the Lord will be your everlasting light, and your days of mourning shall be ended.

to grant to those who mourn in Zion— to give them a beautiful headdress instead of ashes, the oil of gladness instead of mourning, the garment of praise instead of a faint spirit; that they may be called oaks of righteousness, the planting of the Lord, that he may be glorified.
O daughter of my people, put on sackcloth, and roll in ashes; make mourning as for an only son, most bitter lamentation, for suddenly the destroyer will come upon us.
No one shall break bread for the mourner, to comfort him for the dead, nor shall anyone give him the cup of consolation to drink for his father or his mother.
Then shall the young women rejoice in the dance, and the young men and the old shall be merry. I will turn their mourning into joy; I will comfort them, and give them gladness for sorrow.
The joy of our hearts has ceased; our dancing has been turned to mourning.
Sigh, but not aloud; make no mourning for the dead. Bind on your turban, and put your shoes on your feet; do not cover your lips, nor eat the bread of men.

Therefore thus says the Lord, the God of hosts, the Lord: 'In all the squares there shall be wailing, and in all the streets they shall say, Alas! Alas! They shall call the farmers to mourning and to wailing those who are skilled in lamentation,'

I will turn your feasts into mourning and all your songs into lamentation; I will bring sackcloth on every waist and baldness on every head; I will make it like the mourning for an only son and the end of it like a bitter day.
For this I will lament and wail; I will go stripped and naked; I will make lamentation like the jackals, and mourning like the ostriches.

cross_registry_links.note: chil (H2427B) is in the chul root family, which also contains terms owned by anguish (registry 005).
mourning (e.vel H0060) appears in the sorrow field but has strong thematic and lexical overlap with grief (registry 071).
lamentation (a.niy.yah H0592) overlaps thematically with the grief word set.
evil:trouble (a.ven H0205G) was previously held in distress (registry 051) under a different Strong's number assignment. Source file confirms H0205G as the sorrow-owned number.

data_quality_flags.description: Source header lists both 'agony (chi.lah - H2427B)' and 'agony (chil - H2427B)' as if two terms; STEP word analysis block treats them as one entry under H2427B. Treated as single term here. The chi.lah form is noted in also_spelled.
ro.a occurs about 684 times. Only a sample of approximately 35 verses is recorded in the source file. Assessments based on this verse set may not be representative of the full occurrence range.

Source file shows two variant Strong's entries for a.ven: H0205G (evil:trouble) and H0205H (evil:wickedness / also means trouble). Both are recorded in the same word analysis block. H0205G is used as the primary term_id per source heading. H0205H noted in also_spelled.

MTI pre-populated with sorrow (registry 154) terms prior to STEP extraction, using provisional Strong's numbers. 13 of 25 term numbers in the MTI did not match source file values. Source file numbers are authoritative. MTI corrected at end of this session.
